name: footer-section
role: Top-level scope for the marketing footer. Polymorphic <footer role='contentinfo'>. Hosts brand block, link grid, optional newsletter, and legal row. Four canonical variants — minimal (just legal row + brand wordmark), compact (brand + 2-column link grid + legal), default (brand + 4-column link grid + legal), mega (brand + 5-6 column link grid + newsletter + locale picker + legal row, used on flagship marketing sites and docs landings).

semanticStructure: <footer role='contentinfo'> — the landmark; only one per page
<div class='footer-top'> — host for brand block + link grid + newsletter (in mega) at desktop, stacked at mobile
<div class='footer-bottom'> — host for legal row + locale picker + back-to-top link
<nav aria-label='Footer'> — link grid is a navigation landmark; each <footer-link-group> is a <ul> under this nav

variants.minimal: Wordmark + 1-line tagline + legal row. No link grid. Used on auth pages, in-app modals, and minimal product surfaces where the footer should not steal focus.
variants.compact: Brand block (logo + tagline + social) + 2-column link grid + legal row. Used on signup flows, secondary marketing pages, blog post layouts.
variants.default: Brand block + 4-column link grid + legal row. Canonical marketing footer. Used on most marketing pages, docs landings, product launch pages.
variants.mega: Brand block + 5-6 column link grid + newsletter signup + locale picker + extended legal row. Used on the flagship marketing home, /pricing, and high-conversion landing pages where the footer absorbs as much SEO + sub-navigation surface as possible.

newsletterFlow.submitting: data-state='newsletter-submitting' on footer-section. The newsletter button's [aria-busy] microstate engages via action-button. Email input is [readonly].
newsletterFlow.success: data-state='newsletter-success'. Newsletter form is replaced with a success message anchored to the same DOM node. aria-live='polite' announces 'Subscribed — check your inbox to confirm.'
newsletterFlow.error: data-state='newsletter-error'. The form-control type='email' enters its invalid state. Error message rendered below input. aria-live='assertive' announces the error.

ariaPatterns.landmarkSemantics: <footer> with role='contentinfo' is the canonical Page Footer landmark. Only one per page.
ariaPatterns.navLandmark: Link grid is wrapped in <nav aria-label='Footer'>. Each column gets a heading (h3 or h4 depending on host page heading hierarchy).
ariaPatterns.backToTopLink: If present, <a href='#top' aria-label='Back to top'> with optional ↑ glyph. Focus management: clicking it focuses the first focusable element on the page.
ariaPatterns.currentPageMarker: If a footer link points to the current page, render with aria-current='page' + visual underline.

name: footer-link-group
role: Single column of footer links with a category heading. The atomic unit of the footer link grid. Renders as <div> containing <h3> (or h4 / h5 depending on context) + <ul> + <li> + <a>. Multiple footer-link-group instances stack horizontally in the parent footer-section's grid.

semanticStructure: <div> — wrapper (or nav if standalone; but inside footer-section it's a div under the parent <nav>)
<h3> (or h4) — category label, rendered uppercase tracking-wide at type-size-xs (caps-with-letter-spacing aesthetic)
<ul> — flat list of links; no nested sublists (footer copy depth is always 1)
<li> — single link row
<a> — the link; aria-current='page' if it matches the current URL
meta.linkCount: 3-7 links per column is the canonical range. Below 3 looks sparse; above 7 wraps awkwardly at narrow widths.
meta.headingHierarchy: Default to <h3>. If the host page's <main> uses h2 for section headings, downshift footer headings to h4 so screen-reader outline stays sensible.

ariaPatterns.currentPageMarker: <a aria-current='page'> when the link's href matches the current page. Visual treatment: underline + --text-emphasis colour.
ariaPatterns.externalLinks: External links (different origin) get rel='noopener noreferrer' and an optional aria-label='Opens in new tab' if target='_blank'.
ariaPatterns.iconOnlyLinks: Social icon links must have aria-label naming the destination (e.g. 'Quoin on GitHub').

name: footer-brand-block
role: Brand identity area in the footer. Hosts logo / wordmark + tagline + social icon row + optional language/region picker. Renders as <div> containing brand identity, value-proposition tagline, and social/auxiliary controls. Sits as the leftmost column (desktop) or top stack (mobile) in the footer-section's main grid.

semanticStructure: <div> — wrapper
<a href='/' aria-label='Quoin home'> — logo link; SVG wordmark or icon-mark inside
— short value proposition (1-2 sentences, max ~120 chars)
<ul class='social' aria-label='Social media'> — flat list of icon-only links to social profiles (GitHub, X, Mastodon, RSS, LinkedIn, Bluesky as relevant)

<button class='locale-picker'> — if show-locale=true, opens a popover with language/region options. Composes <action-button> from button-system with intent=ghost; popover content via separate primitive (out of scope for v1).
meta.socialIconList: Each <a> in the social list is icon-only — must have aria-label naming the destination platform. Icon ref via icon pack (e.g. icon name='social-github'). Default visible set: GitHub + X + Mastodon + RSS. Override per host.

ariaPatterns.logoLink: <a href='/' aria-label='Quoin home'> wires the wordmark/logo to the home destination. Screen readers announce 'Quoin home, link' rather than 'image, link'.
ariaPatterns.socialNav: <ul aria-label='Social media'> on the social list gives the sublist a label distinguishing it from the main footer navigation.

name: footer-newsletter
role: Inline newsletter signup form in the footer. Composes <form-control type='email'> from @quoin/pattern-form-fields + <action-button intent='primary'> from @quoin/pattern-button-system. Used in the mega footer variant primarily. Renders as a <form> with inline label + email input + submit button + helper text. Handles three submission states (submitting / success / error) via [aria-busy] + aria-live regions.

semanticStructure: <form aria-label='Newsletter signup' method='POST' action='/subscribe'> — outer form; aria-label distinguishes it from other forms on the page
<label for='footer-email'> — visible label; 'Get the Quoin newsletter' or similar
<form-control type='email' id='footer-email' name='email' required> — email input from pattern-form-fields; inherits 33 tokens + leading/trailing slot system + all 8 microstates
<action-button intent='primary' size='md' type='submit'> — submit button from pattern-button-system; receives loading state via [aria-busy] during submission
— small print: 'One email per week. Unsubscribe anytime.' Below the form.
<div role='status' aria-live='polite'> — success/error message region. Empty by default; populated on form response.

registers.stacked: label / input / button arranged vertically. Used in 6-col mega footers where horizontal space is tight, or in mobile breakpoint regardless of desktop register.
registers.inline: label above; input + button on one row with input flex-growing. Used in 4-col mega footers and on wider variants.

submissionFlow.submitting: On form submit: data-state='submitting'. Set [aria-busy=true] on form. Input becomes [readonly]. Button shows loading microstate (delegate to action-button's loading state).
submissionFlow.success: data-state='success'. Form contents replaced (or hidden) with success message in --success colour: 'Subscribed — check your inbox.' aria-live region announces the same.
submissionFlow.error: data-state='error'. Form-control enters [aria-invalid=true] state. Error message rendered in --critical colour. aria-live='assertive' announces 'Subscription failed — [reason].'

ariaPatterns.formLabel: <form aria-label='Newsletter signup'> distinguishes from other site forms.
ariaPatterns.liveRegion: <div role='status' aria-live='polite'> for non-critical success; aria-live='assertive' for errors. Live regions are present in DOM at all states (empty when no message).
ariaPatterns.inputErrorLink:
If validation fails server-side, the error message links back to the input via aria-describedby pointing to the error

.

name: footer-legal-row
role: Bottom strip of the footer. Hosts copyright notice, legal-page links (privacy / terms / cookies / accessibility / DPA / security), locale picker (if not in brand block), and optional back-to-top link. Renders as a <div> with horizontal flex layout (desktop) or stacked (mobile). Smaller typography than the main footer body; muted colour.

semanticStructure: <div class='legal-row'> — wrapper with top border
— '© 2026 Quoin. All rights reserved.' or similar; year should be dynamic
<ul class='legal-links' aria-label='Legal'> — flat list of legal-page links: Privacy / Terms / Cookies / Accessibility / DPA / Security. Inline-flex layout.
<a href='#top' class='back-to-top' aria-label='Back to top'> — if show-back-to-top=true; rendered as text + ↑ glyph
<button class='locale-picker'> — if show-locale=true and not in brand block

registers.compact: Copyright + 2-3 essential legal links (privacy / terms) on one line.
registers.default: Copyright + 4-5 legal links (privacy / terms / cookies / accessibility) on one line.
registers.extended: Copyright + full legal suite (privacy / terms / cookies / accessibility / DPA / security) + locale picker + back-to-top, possibly across 2 visual lines.

ariaPatterns.legalNav: <ul aria-label='Legal'> distinguishes the legal nav from the main footer nav.
ariaPatterns.backToTopLink: <a href='#top' aria-label='Back to top'>; on click, focuses the page's <header> or first focusable element to maintain keyboard context.
ariaPatterns.dynamicYear: Copyright year should be rendered from a JS-set value or server-side template variable; never hardcode.
